name: auth
context: Authenticate with Linear using personal API keys or OAuth 2.0 access tokens
info: Linear supports two authentication methods: personal API keys for scripts and local tools, and OAuth 2.0 for published integrations. Both authenticate via the `Authorization` HTTP header against the GraphQL endpoint at https://api.linear.app/graphql. API keys are generated in Linear Settings → Security → API and sent as a bare token. OAuth tokens use Bearer format. OAuth follows the standard authorization code flow with PKCE support. Agents need this context before making any Linear API call.
body:
# Linear Authentication

Every Linear API call requires authentication via the `Authorization` HTTP header. There are two methods.

## Personal API Keys

For scripts, CLI tools, and agent automation:

1. Open Linear → Settings → Security → API
2. Click **Create key** and give it a label
3. Copy the key — it is shown only once

Use the key directly in the Authorization header:

```bash
curl -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: lin_api_xxxxxxxxxxxx" \
  -d '{"query": "{ viewer { id name email } }"}'
```

With the SDK:

```typescript
import { LinearClient } from "@linear/sdk";

const client = new LinearClient({
  apiKey: process.env.LINEAR_API_KEY,
});

const me = await client.viewer;
console.log(me.name);
```

## OAuth 2.0

For published integrations that act on behalf of users:

### 1. Register your application

Create an OAuth application in Linear Settings → API → OAuth Applications. You'll receive a **client ID** and **client secret**.

### 2. Authorization request

Redirect users to:

```
https://linear.app/oauth/authorize?
  client_id=YOUR_CLIENT_ID&
  redirect_uri=https://your-app.com/callback&
  response_type=code&
  scope=read,write&
  state=random_state_string
```

**Available scopes:**
- `read` — Read access to all resources
- `write` — Write access to all resources
- `issues:create` — Create issues only
- `admin` — Admin access to workspace settings

### 3. Exchange code for token

```bash
curl -X POST https://api.linear.app/oauth/token \
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
  -d 'grant_type=authorization_code&
      client_id=YOUR_CLIENT_ID&
      client_secret=YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET&
      redirect_uri=https://your-app.com/callback&
      code=AUTHORIZATION_CODE'
```

Response:

```json
{
  "access_token": "lin_oauth_xxxxxxxxxxxx",
  "token_type": "Bearer",
  "expires_in": 315360000,
  "scope": "read,write"
}
```

### 4. Use the token

```typescript
import { LinearClient } from "@linear/sdk";

const client = new LinearClient({
  accessToken: process.env.LINEAR_OAUTH_ACCESS_TOKEN,
});
```

Or in raw HTTP:

```bash
curl -X POST https://api.linear.app/graphql \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer lin_oauth_xxxxxxxxxxxx" \
  -d '{"query": "{ viewer { id name } }"}'
```

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose | Method |
|----------|---------|--------|
| `LINEAR_API_KEY` | Personal API key | API Key |
| `LINEAR_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID` | OAuth application client ID | OAuth |
| `LINEAR_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET` | OAuth application client secret | OAuth |
| `LINEAR_OAUTH_ACCESS_TOKEN` | OAuth access token | OAuth |

## Security Notes

- API keys have the same permissions as the user who created them
- Store keys in environment variables or secret managers, never in code
- OAuth tokens can be scoped to limit access
- Rotate API keys periodically via Settings → Security → API
- API keys prefixed with `lin_api_`, OAuth tokens with `lin_oauth_`

sections.title: Personal API keys
sections.body: Generated in Linear Settings → Security → API. Sent as a bare token in the `Authorization` header (no Bearer prefix). Keys have the same permissions as the creating user. Prefix: `lin_api_`. Use `LINEAR_API_KEY` environment variable. Best for: scripts, CLI tools, agent automation, local development.

sections.title: OAuth 2.0 flow
sections.body: Standard authorization code flow. Register an OAuth app in Settings → API → OAuth Applications to get client ID and secret. Authorization URL: `https://linear.app/oauth/authorize`. Token endpoint: `https://api.linear.app/oauth/token`. Scopes: `read`, `write`, `issues:create`, `admin`. Tokens use Bearer format in the Authorization header. Best for: published integrations, multi-user apps, third-party tools.

sections.title: Security best practices
sections.body: Never commit API keys or secrets to source control. Use environment variables or a secret manager. Rotate API keys periodically. Prefer OAuth with minimal scopes for production integrations. API keys inherit the creating user's full permissions — use service accounts for automation when possible.

links.context: Linear ecosystem overview and data model
Create and manage issues after authenticating

related.note: GitHub uses PATs and OAuth similarly
